text:
From: Jin Park <jin.park@kestrelretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2027 18:05:12 -0700
Subject: Streaming sawtooth p99 regression tonight — urgent

Camila / Redwood team,

We're seeing a pronounced p99 spike and a sawtooth pattern on our SSE chat traffic that started ~17:40 PT tonight. Symptoms: p95 is up ~30%, p99 up from ~600ms to ~2.2s, and a growing rate of 502/504 errors when connections recycle. Long-lived streams (5–10+ minute sessions) are timing out or reconnecting repeatedly. We also observed bursts of 429s on the /v1/streams endpoint.

Attached is a short trace and a small log excerpt (kestrel-sse-tail-sample.txt). Key log snippets show repeated \"kv cache miss -> rehydrate\" messages and fast oscillations in request queue depth. This is affecting our checkout chat widgets on the mobile app (high traffic right now).

Can you confirm whe...

Thanks for the productive demo last week. Legal and procurement reviewed Redwood’s standard SLO/credit model and asked us to propose additional contractual protections for a healthcare deployment. High level asks:

- Escrowed holdback equal to 3% of ACV (to be released after 12 months unless material breach)
- Tiered liquidated amounts for repeat Severity 1 incidents (first occurrence: credit only; second occurrence within 12 months: 25% of holdback payable; third occurrence: termination right and remaining holdback paid to customer)
- Immediate termination right for any continuity event >72 hours impacting >60% of traffic

text:
From: Nova Labs <ops@novalabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 08:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: production degradation - model v5.2 tail latency

Hi Ben,

Quick note from our ops team — since late yesterday (09/13) we've seen sustained tail latency increases on the autocomplete path that uses model v5.2. Our telemetry shows p95 rising from ~0.9s to ~1.6s and p99 spikes to ~3s during peak user bursts. This is affecting interactive UX for a small but growing subset of users.

Can Redwood route our production traffic temporarily to the last-known-good variant (v5.1) or enable the fallback routing you mentioned in the onboarding guide? We need a mitigation in place while you investigate the regression. If a short SLA carveout is required so we can accept degraded throughput for a brief window, please advise the steps and any reporting you need from us.

Attac...

Hi Aisha —

We started seeing a sharp jump in p99 latency and a correlated rise in 500 responses for the embeddings route around 08:30 PDT. Throughput dropped by ~30% for a 15m window and then oscillated. Our job runs that depend on timely embeddings are backlogged and a downstream ingestion task failed.

Attached is a snapshot from our Grafana (metric_snapshot-20270912.png) and a short log excerpt. We rolled no code on our side in the window. Can you confirm this is on Redwood's side and give an ETA for mitigation? We will escalate internally if this continues.

Thanks,
Eli

Initial model recommendations (based on today's data profile)
- Candidate A (open-llm-13b-quant): good baseline for intent + extraction; low cost when quantized.

Subject: Recap + proposed perf validation plan (dedicated capacity)

Hi Sophie / Mike —

Thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and propose next steps so we keep momentum:

Key SLOs you gave us on the call:
- p95 generation latency: <= 150 ms for streaming responses (token-by-token)
- sustained concurrency target: 1,200 simultaneous active sessions
- peak sustained throughput target: ~60k tokens/sec (aggregate across pool)
- typical response size: median 60 tokens, 95th percentile up to 800 tokens

Constraints / notes you mentioned:
- majority of requests are streaming-rich (progressive partial tokens) and low CPU in pre/post ...
